---
title: "UIAO MS SQL Estate Inventory Schema"
subtitle: "Schema Reference · mssql-inventory.schema.json"
date: 2026-05-18
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Developer Reference

This is a UIAO JSON Schema, rendered as a developer-reference page per
[ADR-072](../../adr/adr-072-canon-publication-policy.qmd).

- **Source:** [`src/uiao/schemas/mssql-inventory/mssql-inventory.schema.json`](../../../src/uiao/schemas/mssql-inventory/mssql-inventory.schema.json)
- **Dialect:** `http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#`
- **`$id`:** `https://uiao.gov/schemas/mssql-inventory/mssql-inventory.schema.json`
:::

## Description

Normalized output of `MSSQLInventoryAdapter` — the read-only Tier-A1 conformance adapter that discovers and attributes the Microsoft SQL Server estate across an agency tenant. One claim per discovered SQL instance.

Tier-A1 scope is the WHERE and the WHO: instance topology (AD-joined or Azure-resident), ownership cascade per UIAO_153 / ADR-063 (owning principal → host → ARM tag → `ORG-BRANCH-UNPOSITIONED`), and OrgPath attribution. The schema deliberately stops at the boundary of inside-the-instance enumeration (databases, schemas, tables, row counts) — that is Tier-A2, specified separately as a DM_090 amendment and requiring a per-instance read-only SQL login (`VIEW SERVER STATE` + `VIEW ANY DEFINITION`).

Discovery covers five surfaces via two sources:

| Source | Resource family |
|---|---|
| AD LDAP — `MSSQLSvc/*` SPN scan | All Kerberos-registered SQL Server instances (Windows + Linux + AlwaysOn AGs) |
| Azure Resource Graph (read-only) | `microsoft.sql/servers`, `microsoft.sql/servers/databases`, `microsoft.sql/managedinstances`, `microsoft.sql/managedinstances/databases`, `microsoft.azurearcdata/sqlserverinstances`, `microsoft.sqlvirtualmachine/sqlvirtualmachines` |

Companion to the rationalization process spec at `inbox/Modernization/sql-modernization-research/2026-05-17-orgpath-mssql-estate-rationalization-process.md` (Phase 1, "Estate Enumeration with OrgPath Attribution").

## Top-Level Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `adapter_id` | const: `mssql-inventory` | ✓ | Adapter identifier; pins this payload to the canonical adapter. |
| `evaluated_at` | string (date-time) | ✓ | ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the discovery run. |
| `tenant_id` | string \| null |   | Entra tenant ID for the Azure-side discovery; null if AD-only run. |
| `cloud` | enum: `commercial`, `gcc-high`, `dod` |   | Azure cloud boundary the ARG query targeted; aligns with `_graph_clouds.resolve_graph_base`. |
| `claims` | array&lt;`$ref: #/definitions/MSSQLInstanceClaim`&gt; | ✓ | Discovered SQL instances, one claim per instance. |
| `drift_summary` | `$ref: #/definitions/DriftSummary` |   | Aggregate drift findings emitted by `detect_drift()`. |

## `MSSQLInstanceClaim` Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `identifier` | string | ✓ | Canonical identifier — `host\instance` for AD-discovered; ARM resource id for cloud-discovered. |
| `source` | enum (5 values) | ✓ | `ad-spn`, `arg-azure-sql`, `arg-managed-instance`, `arg-arc-sql`, `arg-sql-on-vm`. |
| `host` | string \| null |   | Host FQDN (AD-discovered) or null (cloud-discovered). |
| `port` | integer \| null |   | TCP port (1–65535). |
| `instance_name` | string \| null |   | Named-instance identifier or resource name. |
| `owning_principal` | string \| null |   | SPN principal (AD-discovered); null for cloud (identity resolves via managed identity). |
| `orgpath` | string | ✓ | Cascaded OrgPath per UIAO_153 / ADR-063. Pattern matches the UIAO_151 codebook or the `ORG-BRANCH-UNPOSITIONED` sentinel. |
| `orgpath_attribution_source` | enum | ✓ | `principal-extension`, `host-extension`, `arm-tag`, or `unpositioned`. |
| `azure_resource_id` | string \| null |   | Full ARM id for cloud-discovered instances. |
| `azure_resource_type` | string \| null |   | ARM type (e.g., `microsoft.sql/servers`). |
| `azure_subscription_id` | string \| null |   | ARM subscription id. |
| `azure_location` | string \| null |   | Azure region. |
| `discovered_at` | string (date-time) | ✓ | ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when this instance was observed. |

## `DriftSummary` Properties

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|:---:|---|
| `adapter_id` | string |   | Adapter that emitted the findings. |
| `evaluated_claims` | integer (≥ 0) | ✓ | Total instances evaluated. |
| `findings_count` | integer (≥ 0) | ✓ | Number of drift findings produced. |
| `findings` | array&lt;finding&gt; | ✓ | One entry per claim that violated policy. |

Each finding carries:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `claim_id` | string | The claim that triggered the finding. |
| `drift_class` | enum | One of `DRIFT-IDENTITY`, `DRIFT-PROVENANCE`, `DRIFT-SCHEMA`, `DRIFT-SEMANTIC`, `DRIFT-AUTHZ`. |
| `severity` | enum | `P1`–`P4`. |
| `reason` | string | Machine-readable reason code. |
| `orgpath` | string | The offending OrgPath value (when applicable). |

## Drift conditions surfaced (Tier-A1 scope)

| Class | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `DRIFT-IDENTITY` | Instance has no resolvable OrgPath — cascades to `ORG-BRANCH-UNPOSITIONED`. |
| `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` | ARM tag `OrgPath` present but does not match the UIAO_151 codebook regex. |

The rationalization-specific drift classes (`DRIFT-SSOT-CONTENTION`, `DRIFT-RATIONALIZATION-SCHEMA-OVERLAP`, etc.) are emitted by a sibling analytics module that consumes this adapter's output plus historical baselines. See the rationalization process spec §7 for the full drift surface.

## Full Schema

```{.json filename="mssql-inventory.schema.json"}
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "$id": "https://uiao.gov/schemas/mssql-inventory/mssql-inventory.schema.json",
  "title": "MS SQL Estate Inventory — Tier-A1 Output Schema",
  "description": "Normalized output of MSSQLInventoryAdapter (read-only AD + Azure Resource Graph). One claim per discovered SQL instance. Tier-A1 scope: no inside-the-instance content. Companion to the rationalization process spec at inbox/Modernization/sql-modernization-research/2026-05-17-orgpath-mssql-estate-rationalization-process.md.",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["adapter_id", "evaluated_at", "claims"],
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "adapter_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "const": "mssql-inventory"
    },
    "evaluated_at": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time"
    },
    "tenant_id": { "type": ["string", "null"] },
    "cloud": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["commercial", "gcc-high", "dod"]
    },
    "claims": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": { "$ref": "#/definitions/MSSQLInstanceClaim" }
    },
    "drift_summary": { "$ref": "#/definitions/DriftSummary" }
  }
}
```

The complete schema (including `definitions/MSSQLInstanceClaim` and `definitions/DriftSummary` with their full property constraints, enum members, and pattern matches) lives at [`src/uiao/schemas/mssql-inventory/mssql-inventory.schema.json`](../../../src/uiao/schemas/mssql-inventory/mssql-inventory.schema.json). The page above abbreviates the top-level structure; the source file is the canonical definition and the only one validated by the substrate's schema-validation gate.
